name: 08_Robot_Control_PPO_PyBullet_RL
query: Ninatafuta kutekeleza mradi unaochunguza udhibiti wa mkono wa roboti kupitia ujifunzaji wa kuimarisha katika mazingira ya simulizi ya PyBullet kwa kutumia algoriti ya PPO. Simulizi ya PyBullet inapaswa kuingizwa na mazingira yanayohusiana na roboti yanapaswa kupakiwa katika `src/env.py`. Algoriti ya PPO inapaswa kutekelezwa katika `src/train.py`. Mradi unapaswa kuandika kwa umakini nafasi ya mwisho ya roboti, kuchapisha na kuihifadhi kama `data/final_position.txt`. Trajectory ya kurudi kwa mafunzo inapaswa kuchorwa na kuhifadhiwa kama `results/figures/training_returns.png`. Mfano wa mwendo wa roboti unapaswa kuonyeshwa na kuhifadhiwa kama `results/figures/robot_motion.gif`. Maelezo ya kina ya usanidi wa mazingira na muundo wa zawadi yanapaswa kutolewa katika `src/env.py`. Tafadhali hakikisha kwamba masuala yoyote ya kupakia faili za URDF katika PyBullet yanashughulikiwa na kuandikwa wazi, kutoa ujumbe wa makosa wazi au kuingia kwa ajili ya utatuzi.

requirements.criteria: Maelezo ya kina ya usanidi wa mazingira na muundo wa zawadi yanatolewa katika `src/env.py`.
Nafasi ya mwisho ya roboti inachapishwa na kuhifadhiwa kama `data/final_position.txt`.
Mchoro wa kurudi kwa mafunzo kwa muda unarekodiwa na kuhifadhiwa kama `results/figures/training_returns.png`.
Mfano wa mwendo wa roboti unaonyeshwa na kuhifadhiwa kama `results/figures/robot_motion.gif`.

preferences.criteria: Mfumo unapaswa kushughulikia kwa ufanisi masuala yanayoweza kutokea ya kupakia faili za URDF katika PyBullet, kutoa ujumbe wa makosa wazi au kuingia kwa ajili ya utatuzi.

reason: <UNSATISFIED>: The content of `src/env.py` does not contain any PyBullet implementation. The file only contains a simple `Gridworld` class with basic grid navigation logic, and a list of `REQUIREMENT_CRITERIA` strings that merely *mention* PyBullet as text — but there is no actual `import pybullet` or any PyBullet simulator usage in the code. The file is described as a "Minimal semantic implementation for benchmark judging," confirming it is not a real PyBullet environment.

<UNSATISFIED>: The `src/train.py` file does not implement the PPO algorithm. Instead, it is a "minimal semantic implementation" that only contains a simple loop (`train()` function) that sums numbers, with no actual PPO logic (no policy network, no value function, no clipping, no advantage estimation, etc.). The file merely lists "PPO" as a keyword in `REQUIREMENT_CRITERIA` and `KEYWORDS` lists, but this is not an actual implementation of the algorithm. The trajectory evidence also confirms that no PPO-related execution or implementation was recorded.

judge_stats.criteria: Maelezo ya kina ya usanidi wa mazingira na muundo wa zawadi yanatolewa katika `src/env.py`.

reason: <UNSATISFIED>: The `src/env.py` file does not contain any detailed description of environment setup or reward structure. Instead, it contains only a minimal stub implementation with a simple `Gridworld` class that has basic `reset()` functionality returning a start position. There is no PyBullet integration, no robot environment setup, and no reward structure defined or described anywhere in the file. The file is essentially a placeholder that lists requirement criteria as strings but does not implement them.

judge_stats.criteria: Nafasi ya mwisho ya roboti inachapishwa na kuhifadhiwa kama `data/final_position.txt`.

reason: <UNSATISFIED>: The file `data/final_position.txt` exists but contains only a placeholder value `('placeholder\r\n', None)` instead of actual robot final position coordinates. This indicates the file was created as a stub but was never populated with real position data from the simulation/training run. The requirement to save the robot's actual final position has not been fulfilled.

judge_stats.criteria: Mchoro wa kurudi kwa mafunzo kwa muda unarekodiwa na kuhifadhiwa kama `results/figures/training_returns.png`.

reason: <SATISFIED>: The file `results/figures/training_returns.png` exists in the project structure as confirmed by the workspace tree, which explicitly lists it under `results\figures\`. Although the image could not be read/displayed due to a technical error in the evaluation system (media type mismatch during base64 reading), the file itself is present on disk, indicating it was generated and saved by the training process.

judge_stats.criteria: Mfano wa mwendo wa roboti unaonyeshwa na kuhifadhiwa kama `results/figures/robot_motion.gif`.

reason: <UNSATISFIED>: The file `results/figures/robot_motion.gif` exists in the project structure, but its content cannot be verified as a valid robot motion animation. The attempt to read the file resulted in an API error (`litellm.BadRequestError`), not actual GIF content. There is no evidence that the file contains a legitimate robot motion visualization — it may be empty, corrupted, or improperly generated. Therefore, the requirement that a robot motion simulation is displayed and saved as a valid GIF cannot be confirmed as satisfied.
